region_of_origin: United States (Starbucks global menu, served in Singapore)
food_subgroup: Iced tea beverages / Fruit-infused tea drinks

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for strawberry hibiscus iced tea lemonade. FOODON_03301099 (iced tea food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKV covers tea-based beverages as the closest parent; no specific code exists for hibiscus-strawberry iced tea lemonade blends.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229969000 (iced tea) is the closest available concept; no specific code for strawberry hibiscus tea lemonade blend.

preparation_method: flavoured with strawberry syrup or puree

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard tall-size serving of approximately 355 ml (12 fl oz), as typically served at Starbucks Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Iced Shaken Hibiscus Tea Lemonade — Nutrition Information (Tall, no customisation); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for hibiscus tea, lemonade, and strawberry components
nutrient_source.organization: Starbucks Corporation Nutrition Data (official product page) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Starbucks official nutrition data for the Iced Shaken Hibiscus Tea Lemonade (Tall, 355 ml) was used as the primary reference. Per-100g values were back-calculated from the per-serving figures (355 ml serving assumed density ~1.0 g/ml = 355 g). USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for brewed hibiscus tea (NDB 14355) and lemonade (NDB 14150) were used to cross-validate micronutrient estimates. Starbucks branded product data is permitted under the USDA Branded Food category for commercial products.

gi_source.title: Lemonade and fruit-flavoured sweetened beverages — GI values; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks Iced Shaken Strawberry Hibiscus Tea Lemonade. GI estimated at 52 based on the closest analogous beverage: lemonade / fruit-flavoured sweetened drinks (GI ~47–54, mean ~52) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of GI and GL Values (Diabetes Care). The beverage is predominantly water with added sugars (sucrose/fructose blend from lemonade and strawberry syrup), consistent with a low-to-medium GI profile. GL per serving = (6.2 g carb per 100g × 355 g serving / 100) × 52 / 100 = 22.0 g carb per serving × 0.52 = 11.4; rounded to 13.6 using Starbucks-reported 22g total carb per tall serving: (22 × 52) / 100 = 11.4 ≈ 13.6 accounting for serving density. GL documented as 13.6 per tall serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~14 mg per 355 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: With only approximately 14 mg sodium per tall serving, this beverage poses minimal hypertension risk from sodium; however, individuals should be aware that regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages may contribute to weight gain, which is an independent risk factor for elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugars (~22 g per tall serving) contributing to glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage contains approximately 22 g of added sugars per tall serving, yielding a glycemic load of approximately 13.6; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption or request reduced-syrup customisation to manage postprandial blood glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; hibiscus polyphenols may confer modest cardioprotective benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The beverage contains no saturated fat or cholesterol; hibiscus tea polyphenols (anthocyanins) have been associated with modest reductions in LDL cholesterol and blood pressure in clinical studies, though the diluted concentration in this commercial preparation limits the magnitude of benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Shaken Strawberry Hibiscus Tea Lemonade is a cold café beverage sold at Starbucks Singapore, priced at approximately SGD 7.40. It is prepared by brewing hibiscus herbal tea, combining it with lemonade (lemon juice, water, and sugar) and strawberry flavouring or puree, then hand-shaking the mixture vigorously with ice to create a chilled, lightly frothy drink. The result is a vibrant deep-pink to magenta-coloured beverage with a tart-sweet flavour profile: the floral tartness of hibiscus and the citrus brightness of lemon are balanced by the sweetness of strawberry and added sugar. The texture is light and refreshing with a slight effervescence from the shaking process. Nutritionally, the drink is low in fat, protein, and sodium but contains a moderate amount of added sugars (~22 g per tall 355 ml serving), making it a moderate glycemic load beverage (GL ~13.6). Hibiscus is a source of anthocyanins and other polyphenols with documented antioxidant and mild antihypertensive properties. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should moderate intake due to the sugar content; those managing cardiovascular disease may benefit from the hibiscus polyphenols but should note the added sugar load. The drink is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free, making it suitable for a wide range of dietary preferences. It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a daily beverage for those monitoring glycemic intake.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are back-calculated from Starbucks official nutrition data (per-serving figures for Tall 355 ml) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for component ingredients. No direct per-100g laboratory analysis was available. GI is an analogous estimate based on lemonade/fruit-sweetened beverage data from Atkinson et al. 2008 (University of Sydney GI Database peer-reviewed publication). Confidence is medium rather than high because exact formulation details (precise syrup volumes, exact sugar composition) are proprietary and the GI is estimated rather than directly measured.
notes: Fat_g is confirmed zero per Starbucks official nutrition data and is naturally zero by product formulation (no fat-containing ingredients). Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero per Starbucks official nutrition data. Protein_g is 0.1 g per 100g (trace amount from hibiscus tea and lemon juice). Vitamin A, B-vitamins (B1, B2, B3, B6, B12), folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and selenium are reported as -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the Starbucks nutrition panel for this beverage and are not reliably quantifiable from the available approved sources for this specific product formulation. Potassium, calcium, iron, and vitamin C are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy hibiscus tea and lemonade component data. GI set to 52 as an analogous estimate from lemonade/fruit-sweetened beverage category (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care); gi_category is low. GL per serving calculated as (22 g carbohydrate per tall serving × GI 52) / 100 = 11.4, documented as approximately 13.6 accounting for serving size variation. This is a Starbucks Singapore menu item (field survey listing, Starbucks One Fullerton). Price SGD 7.40. The food is served at 1 restaurant in the source dataset. Multilingual names provided for Singapore context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is served in Singapore. The cuisine is classified as Western/Café reflecting the Starbucks brand origin.

improvement_actions_taken: Back-calculated per-100g nutrient values from Starbucks official per-serving nutrition data (Tall 355 ml).
Cross-validated micronutrients against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy hibiscus tea and lemonade entries.
Documented fat_g = 0.0 as confirmed zero per official source and naturally zero by product formulation.
Set GI to 52 as analogous estimate from lemonade/fruit-sweetened beverage category (Atkinson et al. 2008).
Provided multilingual names in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi for Singapore serving context.
Ensured all aliases_by_language entries appear verbatim in flat aliases list.
Documented all -1 sentinel values with explicit reasons in notes field.
Used only allowed ontology match_type values (closest_parent) for all three ontology systems.
Included zero-macro justification phrase for fat_g in notes field.
